sketches.mechanism: Option::unwrap on None causes a panic with the message 'called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value' — the act of demanding something be present when it is absent destroys the program
sketches.resonance: Insisting that absence contain what you need is the most common way we break ourselves.

sketches.mechanism: TypeScript's 'never' type represents values that can never occur — a function returning 'never' can never successfully complete, yet the type system allows you to assign it a name and call it
sketches.resonance: We name the thing that cannot happen, and the naming is the only form it ever takes.

sketches.mechanism: A LEFT JOIN preserves every row from the left table even when no match exists on the right, filling the missing side with NULL — the join keeps one party whole while the other dissolves into absence
sketches.resonance: Some relationships preserve you completely while the other person was never really there.
sketches.draft:
SELECT
    self.name,
    other.name
FROM
    people self
LEFT JOIN
    people other
ON
    self.reaching_out = other.reaching_back
WHERE
    other.name IS NULL;
